001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.io;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022
023/**
024 * A wrapper around a {@link Writer} that prevents the underlying writer from being closed.
025 *
026 * <p>
027 * This class wraps an existing {@link Writer} and intercepts the {@link #close()} method,
028 * making it a no-op (except for flushing). All other operations are delegated to the underlying
029 * writer. This is useful when you need to pass a writer to code that will close it, but you
030 * want to keep the underlying writer open for further use.
031 *
032 * <h5 class='section'>Features:</h5>
033 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
034 *    <li>Prevents closing - {@link #close()} flushes but doesn't close the underlying writer
035 *    <li>Transparent delegation - all other operations pass through to the wrapped writer
036 *    <li>Useful for resource management - allows multiple consumers without premature closing
037 * </ul>
038 *
039 * <h5 class='section'>Use Cases:</h5>
040 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
041 *    <li>Passing writers to APIs that close them, but you need to keep the writer open
042 *    <li>Multiple operations on the same writer where intermediate operations might close it
043 *    <li>Resource management scenarios where you control the writer lifecycle
044 *    <li>Wrapping system writers that should not be closed
045 * </ul>
046 *
047 * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
048 * <p class='bjava'>
049 *    <jc>// Wrap a writer that should not be closed</jc>
050 *    FileWriter <jv>fw</jv> = <jk>new</jk> FileWriter(<js>"output.txt"</js>);
051 *    NoCloseWriter <jv>wrapper</jv> = <jk>new</jk> NoCloseWriter(<jv>fw</jv>);
052 *
053 *    <jc>// Pass to code that might close it</jc>
054 *    <jv>someMethod</jv>(<jv>wrapper</jv>);  <jc>// May call close(), but fw remains open</jc>
055 *
056 *    <jc>// Continue using the original writer</jc>
057 *    <jv>fw</jv>.write(<js>"more data"</js>);
058 *    <jv>fw</jv>.close();  <jc>// Close when actually done</jc>
059 * </p>
060 *
061 * <h5 class='section'>Important Notes:</h5>
062 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
063 *    <li>The {@link #close()} method flushes the writer but does not close the underlying writer
064 *    <li>You are responsible for closing the underlying writer when you're done with it
065 *    <li>This wrapper does not prevent resource leaks - ensure the underlying writer is eventually closed
066 * </ul>
067 *
068 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
069 *    <li class='jc'>{@link NoCloseOutputStream} - OutputStream counterpart
070 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauCommonsIO">I/O Package</a>
071 * </ul>
072 */
073@SuppressWarnings({
074   "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
075})
076public class NoCloseWriter extends Writer {
077
078   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
079   private static final String ARG_cbuf = "cbuf";
080   private static final String ARG_str = "str";
081   private static final String ARG_w = "w";
082
083   @SuppressWarnings({
084      "resource" // Intentionally not owned; this wrapper deliberately does not close the underlying writer
085   })
086   private final Writer w;
087
088   /**
089    * Constructor.
090    *
091    * <p>
092    * Creates a new NoCloseWriter that wraps the specified Writer. The wrapper will prevent
093    * the underlying writer from being closed via the {@link #close()} method.
094    *
095    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
096    * <p class='bjava'>
097    *    FileWriter <jv>fw</jv> = <jk>new</jk> FileWriter(<js>"file.txt"</js>);
098    *    NoCloseWriter <jv>wrapper</jv> = <jk>new</jk> NoCloseWriter(<jv>fw</jv>);
099    * </p>
100    *
101    * @param w The Writer to wrap. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
102    */
103   public NoCloseWriter(Writer w) {
104      this.w = assertArgNotNull(ARG_w, w);
105   }
106
107   @Override /* Overridden from Writer */
108   public Writer append(char c) throws IOException {
109      return w.append(c);
110   }
111
112   @Override /* Overridden from Writer */
113   public Writer append(CharSequence csq) throws IOException {
114      return w.append(csq);
115   }
116
117   @Override /* Overridden from Writer */
118   public Writer append(CharSequence csq, int start, int end) throws IOException {
119      return w.append(csq, start, end);
120   }
121
122   /**
123    * Flushes the writer but does not close the underlying Writer.
124    *
125    * <p>
126    * This method flushes any buffered data to the underlying writer but does not close it.
127    * The underlying writer remains open and can continue to be used after this method is called.
128    *
129    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
130    * <p class='bjava'>
131    *    FileWriter <jv>fw</jv> = <jk>new</jk> FileWriter(<js>"file.txt"</js>);
132    *    NoCloseWriter <jv>wrapper</jv> = <jk>new</jk> NoCloseWriter(<jv>fw</jv>);
133    *    <jv>wrapper</jv>.close();  <jc>// Flushes but doesn't close fw</jc>
134    *    <jv>fw</jv>.write(<js>"still works"</js>);  <jc>// fw is still open</jc>
135    * </p>
136    *
137    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs while flushing.
138    */
139   @Override /* Overridden from Writer */
140   public void close() throws IOException {
141      w.flush();
142   }
143
144   @Override /* Overridden from Writer */
145   public void flush() throws IOException {
146      w.flush();
147   }
148
149   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
150   public String toString() {
151      return w.toString();
152   }
153
154   @Override /* Overridden from Writer */
155   public void write(char[] cbuf) throws IOException {
156      assertArgNotNull(ARG_cbuf, cbuf);
157      w.write(cbuf);
158   }
159
160   @Override /* Overridden from Writer */
161   public void write(char[] cbuf, int off, int len) throws IOException {
162      assertArgNotNull(ARG_cbuf, cbuf);
163      w.write(cbuf, off, len);
164   }
165
166   @Override /* Overridden from Writer */
167   public void write(int c) throws IOException {
168      w.write(c);
169   }
170
171   @Override /* Overridden from Writer */
172   public void write(String str) throws IOException {
173      assertArgNotNull(ARG_str, str);
174      w.write(str);
175   }
176
177   @Override /* Overridden from Writer */
178   public void write(String str, int off, int len) throws IOException {
179      assertArgNotNull(ARG_str, str);
180      w.write(str, off, len);
181   }
182}
